stimulus:
You are participating in a study about language and related cognitive abilities being conducted by Professor Evelina Fedorenko from the Department of Brain and Cognitive Sciences at MIT. If you have questions or concerns, you can contact Professor Fedorenko by email (evelina9@mit.edu).

Your participation in this research is voluntary. You may decline to answer any or all of the following questions. You may decline further participation, at any time, without adverse consequences.

Your anonymity is assured; the researchers who have requested your participation will not receive any personal information about you.

The only restriction to being paid is achieving the accuracy requirements as detailed in the task instructions (to block "bots" who answer randomly).

I agree to take part in this study.

Instructions: Sentence Production Experiment

We ask that you try to use complete sentences with a subject and a verb. For example, "A dog is sitting on a porch." instead of "a dog on a porch", or "The children are playing happily." instead of "Happy children". Please use at least 5 words.

Please make sure you understand these instructions and are able to follow them in every question. Do not complete this survey if you do not understand.

You will not get paid if your answers do not follow the instructions (for example, if you write things unrelated to the image or write the same thing such as "a nice picture" for each image).

Please click "next" when you are ready to begin.
